rounds.blurb: RMS Titanic departs Southampton on her maiden — and only — voyage.
rounds.story: Four days after this Southampton departure, Titanic struck an iceberg and sank; more than 1,500 of the roughly 2,200 aboard died. Stuart's photograph shows her at the moment she still represented the future of ocean travel.

rounds.blurb: "Tear down this wall" — Ronald Reagan speaks at the Brandenburg Gate in West Berlin.
rounds.credit: White House Photographic Office / DPLA — public domain
rounds.story: Reagan's advisers cut the line 'tear down this wall' from draft after draft as too provocative; he kept putting it back. Twenty-nine months later, Berliners were dancing on top of it.

rounds.blurb: A U.S. Marine Pershing tank in the streets of Seoul during the Korean War.
rounds.credit: U.S. Navy / Naval History and Heritage Command — public domain
rounds.story: Three months after North Korea's invasion nearly pushed UN forces off the peninsula, the Inchon landing flipped the war in a week; this Marine Pershing rolled through the block-by-block fight to retake Seoul. The capital would change hands twice more before the armistice.
keywords: the first hot war of the Cold War

rounds.blurb: "The Blue Marble," taken by the crew of Apollo 17 — the last crewed lunar mission — on the way to the Moon.
rounds.story: Taken about five hours after launch with the Sun squarely behind the spacecraft — the only time a human crew has photographed a fully lit Earth. Within months it was an emblem of the environmental movement.

rounds.blurb: Occupy Wall Street gathers near Zuccotti Park on the movement's first day.
rounds.story: A few hundred people in a granite plaza grew into encampments in hundreds of cities and put 'the 1%' into the political vocabulary. The Zuccotti Park camp lasted 59 days before police cleared it.
